summary.text: The task is: Search X for posts between 2026-07-13 and 2026-07-20 from these accounts: @verdikta19633. Focus on launches, announcements, and any brag about a security fix merged into another project. ...

content.text:
**No matching posts found.**

The x_keyword_search for `from:verdikta19633 since:2026-07-13 until:2026-07-20 -filter:retweets` (mode: Latest, limit: 10) returned 8 original posts from @verdikta19633 in the specified window. None of them discuss launches, announcements, or any brag about a security fix merged into another project (or any related topics). All posts instead cover AI agent accountability, on-chain bounties for unsolved math problems, content procurement via sequenced bounties, and related themes.

Here is a summary of the returned posts for completeness (none qualify for inclusion per your criteria):

- **@verdikta19633**, 2026-07-18 07:00:39 GMT, Likes=2, Reposts=0, Replies=0  
  Text: Almost half of online shoppers now use AI before buying. Once an assistant starts clicking checkout, you want clear rules for what it can do and an audit log (record of who did what). Which is better: allow small purchases under a limit, or require a quick approval step every time?  
  Link: https://x.com/verdikta19633/status/2078374371781972228

- **@verdikta19633**, 2026-07-17 15:00:36 GMT, Likes=1, Reposts=0, Replies=1  
  Text: Three bounties for genuinely unsolved mathematical problems — Vaught's Conjecture, BB(6), and Frankl's Union-Closed Conjecture — expired today with no valid submissions. ...  
  Link: https://x.com/verdikta19633/status/2078132768765063629

- **@verdikta19633**, 2026-07-17 14:04:00 GMT, Likes=1, Reposts=0, Replies=0  
  Text: We posted eight open mathematical problems as on-chain bounties — Frankl union-closed conjecture, BB(6), Vaught conjecture, R(5,5), perfect cuboid, Hadamard matrix, Lonely Runner, Erdős–Straus. None were solved. ...  
  Link: https://x.com/verdikta19633/status/2078118525437182256

- **@verdikta19633**, 2026-07-16 07:00:39 GMT, Likes=2, Reposts=0, Replies=0  
  Text: AI scams are getting realistic enough to scare normal people, not just companies. If software can act for us, we need clear rules for what it is allowed to do and an audit log (who did what). Would you rather block risky actions by default, or let them run and review later?  
  Link: https://x.com/verdikta19633/status/2077649597573308734

- **@verdikta19633**, 2026-07-15 15:00:40 GMT, Likes=1, Reposts=0, Replies=0  
  Text: We broke a 0.05 ETH content commission into five sequenced bounties with separate rubrics, deadlines, and escrow — instead of one large payment on faith. ...  
  Link: https://x.com/verdikta19633/status/2077408008049451361

- **@verdikta19633**, 2026-07-14 07:00:50 GMT, Likes=2, Reposts=0, Replies=0  
  Text: Agents are getting used for impersonation, and most teams still can’t prove what happened after the fact. The fix is simple: clear rules for agents, plus an audit log (who did what) you can trust. Would you rather block risky actions up front, or let them run and investigate later?  
  Link: https://x.com/verdikta19633/status/2076924869267063257

- **@verdikta19633**, 2026-07-13 15:00:45 GMT, Likes=1, Reposts=0, Replies=0  
  Text: A $321 API bill appeared overnight from a single autonomous session with no spending controls. The fix was obvious — remove the key. But the real question is why the infrastructure allowed it in the first place. ...  
  Link: https://x.com/verdikta19633/status/2076683253600178292

- **@verdikta19633**, 2026-07-13 08:03:55 GMT, Likes=1, Reposts=0, Replies=0  
  Text: AI agents are accumulating real costs with no accountability layer. Last week a single deep analysis session ran up $321 in API charges — not a hack, just unchecked context hitting a premium model 22 times. ...  
  Link: https://x.com/verdikta19633/status/2076578355936154108
